title: Eriophyllum chrysanthemodes
taxonomy: Eriophyllum chrysanthemodes is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family, and is a member of the genus Eriophyllum. The genus Eriophyllum is part of the tribe Madieae, which is further classified within the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. The Asteraceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many common species. Eriophyllum chrysanthemodes is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.
uses: Eriophyllum chrysanthemodes, a shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts have also been investigated for their potential antimicrobial and antifungal activities. However, further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and benefits of Eriophyllum chrysanthemodes.
benefits: Eriophyllum chrysanthemodes, also known as Chrysanthemum flower, is a type of flowering plant that has been studied for its potential health benefits. properties: Eriophyllum chrysanthemodes, also known as California fleabane, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. constituents: Eriophyllum chrysanthemodes contains a variety of active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. parts: Eriophyllum chrysanthemodes is a shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine by various Native American tribes, particularly the Chumash and the Tongva, for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. preparations: Eriophyllum chrysanthemodes has been used in traditional herbal preparations, primarily for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
